commit.message:
TarReader: implement GNU sparse format 1.0 (PAX) (#125283)

`TarReader` was not handling GNU sparse format 1.0 PAX entries, causing
~46% of entries from bsdtar-created archives (e.g., .NET SDK tarballs
built on macOS/APFS) to expose internal placeholder paths like
`GNUSparseFile.0/real-file.dll`, incorrect sizes, and corrupted
extracted content.

## Changes

Added read-only support for GNU sparse format 1.0 (PAX). When
`TarReader` encounters PAX extended attributes `GNU.sparse.major=1` and
`GNU.sparse.minor=0`, it resolves the real file name from
`GNU.sparse.name`, reports the expanded size from `GNU.sparse.realsize`,
and wraps the raw data stream with `GnuSparseStream` which presents the
expanded virtual file content (zeros for holes, packed data at correct
offsets).

The sparse map embedded in the data section is parsed **lazily** on
first `Read`, so `_dataStream` remains unconsumed during entry
construction. This allows `TarWriter.WriteEntry` to round-trip the
condensed sparse data correctly for both seekable and non-seekable
source archives.

Older GNU sparse formats (0.0, 0.1) and write support are not addressed.

Additional correctness and robustness improvements based on code review:
- `GnuSparseStream` now overrides `DisposeAsync` to properly await async
disposal of the underlying raw stream.
- `TarHeader.Read` now throws `InvalidDataException` if
`GNU.sparse.realsize` is negative, consistent with validation of the
regular `_size` field.
- Segment validation uses overflow-safe arithmetic (`offset > _realSize
|| length > _realSize - offset`).
- `FindSegmentFromCurrent` uses binary search (O(log n)) for backward
seeks, preserving the O(1) amortized forward scan for the common
sequential-read case.

```csharp
// Before: entry.Name == "GNUSparseFile.0/dotnet.dll", entry.Length == 512
// After:  entry.Name == "dotnet.dll", entry.Length == 1048576
using var reader = new TarReader(archiveStream);
TarEntry entry = reader.GetNextEntry();
entry.DataStream.ReadExactly(content); // correctly expanded virtual file
```

## Testing

All existing tests pass. New `TarReader.SparseFile.Tests.cs` covers:
- Parameterized sparse layouts (single segment, holes, multiple
segments, all-holes) × `copyData` × sync/async
- Corrupted sparse map handling (non-numeric values, truncated maps,
buffer overflow) × sync/async
- Negative `GNU.sparse.realsize` value throws `InvalidDataException`
(sync and async) — the test helper `WriteSparseEntry` omits
`GNU.sparse.realsize` from the `PaxTarEntry` constructor's attribute
dictionary (to avoid constructor-level validation) and instead injects
it via reflection into the internal `TarHeader.ExtendedAttributes`
dictionary after construction, so the archive can be built while
ensuring `TarReader.GetNextEntry()` is the one that throws
- Wrong sparse version detection (missing minor, wrong major)
- Seekable random access, partial reads, advance-past-entry correctness
- Round-trip copy through TarWriter with seekable/non-seekable source ×
copyData
- Sparse layout scenarios tested against real `golang_tar` test data
files (`pax-nil-sparse-data.tar`, `pax-nil-sparse-hole.tar`,
`pax-sparse-big.tar`) from the `System.Formats.Tar.TestData` NuGet
package, plus programmatically constructed archives for additional
coverage
- Test code refactored to eliminate duplication:
`AdvancePastEntry_DoesNotCorruptNextEntry` and
`CopySparseEntryToNewArchive_PreservesExpandedContent` now share archive
construction helpers (`WriteSparseEntry`, `BuildSparseArchive`,
`BuildRawSparseArchive`) with the rest of the test suite

commit.message:
fix: strip ADR/ADRC/ADS suffixes in _normalise_name to prevent auto-selection

Before this fix, Polygon returns 'Infosys Limited ADR' which normalised to
'infosys adr' — a different key from 'infosys'. This caused two company groups,
routing through _llm_auto_select which silently picked INFY (US) without asking.

After this fix all ADR/ADRC/ADS variants strip to the same key as the base name,
so all exchange listings land in one group. _resolve_single_company is called,
which deduplicates by exchange and presents the interactive menu when multiple
listings exist (NSE/BSE/NYSE).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: rdtect <31343717+rdtect@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: rewrite workflow to use bun instead of npm

The original PR #9 created a Node.js + npm CI pipeline. This project
uses Bun (bun.lock is the authoritative lockfile), so the npm-based
workflow would diverge from local development and potentially catch
different bugs than what developers see.

Changes:
- Replace actions/setup-node@v4 with oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
- Replace `npm ci` with `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- Replace `npm run check/build` with `bun run check/build`
- Remove the `npm audit` step — bun has no equivalent. Rely on
  Dependabot for security scanning instead.
- Hoist `permissions: contents: read` to the workflow level (still
  least-privilege, slightly cleaner YAML).
- Rename job from "Type-check, Build & Security Audit" to
  "Type-check & Build" since audit is no longer part of this job.

The security fixes that motivated the original PR (Vite path traversal,
Rollup arbitrary file write, minimatch/picomatch ReDoS, @sveltejs/kit
form-deserialization DoS) should still land via bun.lock updates.
`bun install` confirmed bun.lock already has the patches.

commit.message:
feat: implement two-phase close handshake for session restore

Implement a proper lifecycle close handshake between Rust and TypeScript
to ensure IndexedDB writes complete before window destruction, fixing
editor tab restoration after session restore (Issue #35).

Rust changes:
- Add PendingCloses with tokio::oneshot for timeout cancellation
- Rewrite CloseRequested handler: api.prevent_close() + emit to TS
- Add lifecycle_close_confirmed command (save session + destroy)
- Add lifecycle_close_vetoed command (cancel timeout, keep window)
- 30s safety-net timeout via tokio::select!

TypeScript changes:
- Rewrite TauriLifecycleService extending AbstractLifecycleService
  directly (not BrowserLifecycleService) for full async veto support
- handleCloseRequested(): orchestrates async veto → shutdown → confirm
- fireBeforeShutdown(): supports InternalBeforeShutdownEvent + finalVeto
- handleShutdown(): await joiners → flush storage → invoke confirmed
- withExpectedShutdown(): BrowserHostService structural compatibility

Closes #35

commit.message:
fix: add `toBuffer` mock to `createMockL2Block` to fix failing unit tests (#603)

* fix: prevent Kafka consumer offset replay loop on un-orphan path

Three fixes for the infinite message replay bug in explorer-api:

1. Add heartbeat() call before handler dispatch in message-bus eachMessage
   to prevent broker from declaring the consumer dead during long DB ops
   and triggering a group rebalance that resets the offset.

2. Replace O(N) serial INSERT loop in ensureParentBlocksFinalizationStatusStored
   with a single bulk INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, eliminating the
   DB stall that caused ~30k round trips per un-orphan event on TESTNET.

3. Remove duplicate ensureFinalizationStatusStored call in the storeBlock
   catch handler — it was already called inside unOrphanCallback, causing
   the expensive ancestor walk to run twice per replayed message.

* Update services/explorer-api/src/svcs/database/controllers/l2block/store.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Glossaire — soulignement pointillé et infobulle latérale pour les termes définis (#59)

Implémente la fonctionnalité glossaire de l'issue CALME : les termes
définis dans un dictionnaire JSON sont soulignés en pointillé
(`text-decoration: underline dotted`) dans le rendu Markdown ; clic →
définition + lien dans le panneau latéral, survol → infobulle après
délai configurable.

## Changements

### Couche pure (`src/core/`)
- **`types.ts`** — interfaces `GlossaryTerm`, `GlossaryConfig`,
`GlossaryMatchingConfig`, `GlossaryUiConfig`
- **`glossary.ts`** — fonctions pures : `buildGlossaryMap`,
`mergeGlossarySources`, `buildTermRegex` (regex Unicode avec flag `u`,
tri longest-first pour éviter les sous-chaînes)

### Adaptateur navigateur (`src/adapters/browser/glossary.ts`)
- Fetch parallèle des sources JSON + merge last-wins par clé
`term.toLowerCase()` / `alias.toLowerCase()`
- Tree-walker DOM sur les nœuds texte ; exclusion configurable (`code`,
`pre`, `kbd`)
- Wrapping en `<span class="ow-term" role="button">` avec gestionnaire
clic → `#ow-glossary-panel` dans `#sidebar`
- Support SVG inline (`<text>/<tspan>`) optionnel via `svg.inline: true`

### Intégration (`src/adapters/browser/enhance.ts`)
```typescript
// Appelé en fin de pipeline afterRender
if (cfg.glossary?.enabled !== false && cfg.glossary?.sources?.length) {
  await applyGlossary(appEl, cfg.glossary)
}
```

### Config (`config.json`)
```json
{
  "glossary": {
    "enabled": true,
    "sources": ["./glossary.fr.json"],
    "matching": { "caseSensitive": false, "wordBoundary": true, "firstOccurrenceOnly": false },
    "exclude": { "elements": ["code", "pre", "kbd"] },
    "ui": { "underlineStyle": "dotted", "clickBehavior": "sidebar", "tooltip": { "showOnHover": true, "delay": 300 } }
  }
}
```

### Démo & tests
- `docs/demos/03-glossary/` — page HTML + `.fr.md` / `.en.md` +
dictionnaires `glossary.{fr,en}.json` avec termes CALME (UAF, AEG,
LGGRI, Capabilité, Gouvernance IT)
- `tests/glossary.test.ts` — 11 tests Vitest sur les fonctions pures
(tous verts)
- `tests/e2e/demos/03-glossary.spec.js` — tests Playwright (annotation
des termes, exclusion `<code>`, clic sidebar, snapshot visuel)

- [x] Convention de commit respectée
- [ ] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK
- [x] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune
- [x] Tests ajoutés ou N/A

⚡ Bolt: Prevent deep proxy performance overhead on Cesium.Viewer (#3)

* ⚡ Bolt: Use $state.raw for Cesium.Viewer and add keys to each blocks

- Replaced `$state` with `$state.raw` for the Cesium viewer instance in `CesiumViewer.svelte`. Deep proxying a massive external 3D engine instance caused severe performance overhead on every internal state change. Svelte 5 best practices dictate using `$state.raw` for large objects that only trigger reactivity on reassignment.
- Added unique keys `(loc.id)` to `{#each}` blocks in `SidePanel.svelte` for optimal surgical DOM updates rather than full array re-rendering.
- Added a learning entry to `.jules/bolt.md`.
